channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: WH lambasts ‘head-in-the-sand’ liberal prosecutors after 20 AGs sue to halt DOGE cuts
item.description: The White House reacted Monday after 20 attorneys general from New Jersey to California joined a Maryland suit seeking to halt DOGE's cuts to the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: "Gross abuses of executive power raise serious concerns over the freedoms of peaceful assembly, expression and association," the group wrote of the U.S. rating.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, NewsNation

item.title: OPM inspector general investigating DOGE emails, access to agency data
item.description: The inspector general for the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) told lawmakers he is investigating the use of a new data system at the agency that has been part of a broader effort by Elon Musk and his allies to email federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump loves the Gilded Age and its tariffs. It was a great time for the rich but not for the many
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In President Donald Trump's idealized framing, the United States was at its zenith in the 1890s, when top hats and shirtwaists were fashionable and typhoid fever often killed more soldiers than combat.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans worry Trump's tariffs could harm economy
item.description: “The Canadian tariffs will definitely have a detrimental impact on the economy of Maine and on border communities in particular,” said Sen. Susan Collins (R-Maine).
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Michelle Obama and her brother to launch a podcast with weekly guests
item.description: Michelle Obama and her brother, Craig Robinson, will host a new weekly podcast series starting this month featuring a special guest pulled from the world of entertainment, sports, health and business.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: EPA froze 'green bank' funds worth billions, climate group suit says
item.description: A nonprofit that was awarded nearly $7 billion by the Biden administration to finance clean energy and climate-friendly projects has sued the Environmental Protection Agency, accusing it of improperly freezing its account.
item.dc:creator: Michael Phillis And Matthew Daly, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dems weaponize Medicaid anxiety in bid to take down Trump-backed federal funding plan
item.description: House Democrats are seizing on concerns over Medicaid and Medicare as a way to oppose Republicans' plan to fund the government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dem governor to headline major fundraiser in key presidential primary state stoking 2028 speculation
item.description: Dem Gov. J.B. Pritzker of Illinois, a leading figure in the Trump opposition, sparks 2028 speculation with news that he will headline major party fundraiser in the key primary state of New Hampshire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sec Rubio says purge of USAID programs complete with 83% gone, remainder now falling under State Dept
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced early Monday morning that USAID had slashed 83% of its programs, following a six week review of its spending by DOGE, and the remaining programs will now be under the purview of the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Trump downplays tariff concerns as US stock sell-off worsens
item.description: President Donald Trump is dismissing business concerns over the uncertainty caused by his planned tariffs on a range of American trading partners and the prospect of higher prices and isn’t ruling out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Noem taps new ICE leaders and moves to identify leakers
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Sunday announced new leadership at the agency tasked with immigration enforcement as she also pledged to step up lie detector tests on employees to identify those who may be leaking information about operations to the media.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Unions ask court to stop DOGE from accessing Social Security data of millions of Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A group of labor unions are asking a federal court for an emergency order to stop Elon Musk 's Department of Government Efficiency from accessing the sensitive Social Security data of millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy — the top issue in the 2024 presidential election — has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: 5 things to watch as Trump delivers joint address to Congress
item.description: President Trump will head to the Capitol on Tuesday for the first joint address of his second term emboldened by a month of mass federal agency slashing, shocking foreign policy moves and a dizzying array of executive actions that have disrupted Washington and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
